meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Version Control for Freelancers (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Use this beginner-friendly checklist to master AI-driven version control with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance output.
content.answer_block: Eliminate 'no version control' chaos by treating your UI design as code. Use Antigravity to generate structured UI components and Claude Code to manage iterative commits. By syncing AI-generated design files directly into a Git-tracked architecture, you transform volatile drafts into a professional, reproducible, and secure development pipeline.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, a client demands a change, and you’re staring at a folder of 'final_v2_real_final.png' files. The lack of version control isn't just an annoyance; it’s a career-limiting bottleneck that bleeds your billable hours and destroys client trust. When your design process is disconnected from your code, you aren't building—you're gambling.

But the tide has turned. By integrating AI-driven design tools with automated CLI agents, you can treat your creative process like a production-grade software project. This isn't about working harder; it's about building a robust, version-controlled architecture that allows you to iterate at the speed of thought while keeping every single change perfectly documented.

steps.title: Initialize the Design Environment
steps.description: Establish a clean repository structure where design tokens and code live in harmony to prevent file fragmentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized project folder structure for a React-based design system, including directories for components, assets, and design-tokens.json.

steps.title: Generate Versioned UI Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate modular components that are easily tracked and versioned as individual files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive button component using Tailwind CSS, ensuring all styles are extracted into a theme object for consistent versioning.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Button.tsx based on design-tokens.json'

steps.title: Automate Commit Documentation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate meaningful commit messages that explain the 'why' behind every design iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current changes in the UI components and write a professional, concise git commit message following conventional commits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: implement accessible primary button with hover states'

steps.title: Implement Branching for Experiments
steps.description: Create feature branches for design variations, allowing you to present options to clients without affecting the main codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new branch strategy for testing three different hero section design variations, ensuring each is isolated for client review.

steps.title: Sync Design Tokens to Production
steps.description: Ensure your design tokens are the source of truth, so a single change in the token file updates the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current project to consume design-tokens.json for all spacing, color, and typography values across all components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix --refactor 'Migrate hardcoded styles to design-tokens.json'

pro_tips: Always commit your design-tokens.json file as a separate early commit to track the evolution of your design system's 'DNA' over time.
Use Claude Code to 'diff' your AI-generated components against the previous iteration to identify unexpected style regressions before pushing.
Tag your releases in Git with semantic versioning (v1.0.0) whenever you send a major design milestone to a client.
Keep your Antigravity prompts in a local 'prompts.md' file inside your repo to ensure consistency across different projects.
Automate your 'design review' process by linking GitHub PRs to Vercel preview deployments so clients can see changes live without manual screenshots.
content.next_steps: Once your first version-controlled project is live, start building a library of reusable 'design-token' templates that you can swap into new client projects to cut your setup time by 80%.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your files and start scaling your freelance business with a professional, automated workflow. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Antigravity to generate modular components that are easily tracked and versioned as individual files.
Use Claude Code to generate meaningful commit messages that explain the 'why' behind every design iteration.
Create feature branches for design variations, allowing you to present options to clients without affecting the main codebase.
Ensure your design tokens are the source of truth, so a single change in the token file updates the entire application.
